value.found_quote: The sandboxed Bash tool leverages operating system security primitives... These OS-level restrictions ensure that all child processes spawned by Claude Code's commands inherit the same security boundaries.
value.rationale: The page is Anthropic's Claude Code documentation describing the sandboxed Bash tool, which provides OS-level filesystem and network isolation for every Bash command and its child processes. It states "Sandboxing provides OS-level enforcement that restricts what Bash commands can access at the filesystem and network level. It applies only to Bash commands and their child processes," and "The sandbox is built into Claude Code." This directly matches the claim. No deprecation or sunset language is present.
